title: How the US Government Plans to Refund Billions in Tariffs
summary: When the Supreme Court struck down President Trump’s tariffs, it triggered a $166 billion refund process. On today’s Big Take podcast: the logistics of that payout and how soon businesses can expect relief.

title: Nagel Favors ECB June Rate Hike Unless Outlook Improves Markedly
summary: The European Central Bank will need to increase interest rates in June if there isn’t a significant change in the outlook, according to Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel.

title: Aluminum Facing 'Very Large Supply Hole,' JPM Says
summary: Greg Shearer, JPMorgan base and precious metals research head, discusses the aluminum market and the impact the conflict in the Middle East on prices. "Our outlook in the very near term is still for prices to move up towards $4,000 per metric ton," Shearer tells Bloomberg Television. "There will be demand destruction at those levels," he adds. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: FTSE 100 Drops in Thin Holiday Trading as NatWest Results Weigh
summary: UK shares dropped in thin holiday trading as NatWest Group Plc fell more than 3% after reporting net interest income that came in slightly below expectations.

title: Yen Resumes Rally Sparked by First Intervention in Two Years
summary: The yen resumed its advance in late afternoon trading in Tokyo after an earlier pause in the rally triggered by Japan’s first intervention in the currency market since 2024.

title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Futures Rise, Yen Pares Gain: Markets Wrap
summary: US equity futures edged higher, signaling that a rally which pushed Wall Street gauges to record highs on strong megacap tech earnings may have further to run. The yen resumed gains triggered by Japan’s intervention to support the currency.

title: Japan Reveals First Loans to US Projects Under Trade Deal
summary: Japan’s government-backed lender and private bankers announced their first loans as part of the nation’s $550 billion commitment to US projects under a trade agreement with President Donald Trump.

title: Iran War: Trump Says Blockade Having 'Incredible' Effect | Daybreak Europe 05/01/2026
summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

On today's show, President Donald Trump has doubled down on the US blockade of Iranian ports, saying it's having an "incredible" effect and Iran's "economy is crashing". Iran's supreme leader has cast doubt on the chances of a deal, releasing a rare statement in which he said Iran would not give up its nuclear technologies.

The Bank of England and European Central Bank both held interest rates, but are considering hiking as soon as June to address inflation triggered by the Middle East conflict.

Today's guests: Jane Kinninmont, United Nations Association UK, CEO; Jackie Bowie, Chatham Financial, Managing Partner & Head of EMEA. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ceres Power Shares Near 1,000% Gain as Fuel Cell Rally Extends
summary: Ceres Power Holdings Plc extended a blistering rally this week, pushing one-year gains close to 1,000% as surging optimism around fuel cell technology drives the stock to multi-year highs.

title: Adani Group Unveils Restructuring as It Positions for Growth
summary: Billionaire Gautam Adani’s conglomerate unveiled plans to overhaul its operating model, in an effort to cut decision-making time as it pushes for accelerated growth across businesses.

title: Ooredoo CEO: Zero Interruptions Due to Regional Crisis
summary: Qatar telco Ooredoo has kicked off the year with steady growth, reporting 1Q revenue up 6% y/y, in line with analyst estimates while profit climbed 4.7%. Group CEO Aziz Aluthman Fakhroo spoke to Bloomberg’s Jennifer Zabasajja on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the numbers and growth strategy despite regional tensions. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Yen’s Correlation With Oil Hit 2021 High Before Intervention
summary: The correlation between dollar-yen and Brent crude rose to its highest level since late 2021 a day before Japanese authorities intervened in the currency market, underscoring the tighter link between oil prices and the yen.

title: ECB Increasingly Likely to Hike in Near Future, Muller Says
summary: The European Central Bank will probably increase borrowing costs if the Iran war persists, according to outgoing Governing Council member Madis Muller.

title: Gold Steady as Dollar Weakens on Reports of Yen Intervention
summary: Gold moved in a tight range after the US dollar retreated sharply on Japan’s likely intervention in the foreign-exchange market, helping to offset heightened inflation risks arising from the Iran war.

title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 5/1/2026
summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: FTSE 100 Falls as NatWest Leads Banks Lower
Copper Steadies After Advance as China Holiday Saps Trading
summary: Copper and other base metals including zinc maintained gains, shrugging off the declines of earlier in the week, as China’s Labor Day holiday kept market activity subdued.

title: Oil Holds Weekly Gain as Trump Says Iran Blockade Is Working
summary: Oil held its second weekly gain as US President Donald Trump said he was sticking with a naval blockade of Iranian ports, elevating concerns the vital Strait of Hormuz would not reopen anytime soon.

title: Singapore PM Warns of AI Disruptions, Vows to Support Workers
summary: Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong warned of bigger disruptions ahead as the country navigates a more uncertain global environment, from the Iran conflict to the rapid rise of artificial intelligence.

title: Record-Low Spreads on EM Asia High-Grade Debt Show Resilience
summary: Yield premiums on emerging-market Asian investment-grade dollar bonds have tightened to unprecedented levels amid reduced new issuance and China’s economic resilience despite Iran-war disruptions.

title: Japan’s Mimura Declines to Comment on Yen Intervention Talk
summary: Japan’s top currency official said authorities in Tokyo are maintaining readiness to intervene in the crude oil futures market, where speculative moves have been affecting the currency.

title: Goldman’s Freund Sees Early Signs of Australia M&A Rebound
summary: Australia’s subdued deals landscape is showing early signs of recovery after lagging Asia and the US, according to Goldman Sachs’ local head of mergers and acquisitions.

title: Yen Soars After Japan Intervened Following ‘Final’ Warning
summary: The yen surged 3%, its biggest gain in almost two years, after Japan intervened in the foreign-exchange market following a “final” warning by officials to investors against selling the currency.

title: Economic Crosscurrents Go Global as Energy Shock Meets AI Wave
summary: The global economy is increasingly captive to two opposing forces.

title: CFTC Reviews Trader Data Report as Kalshi Expands in Commodities
summary: A top Wall Street regulator is considering changes to a weekly report covering the position of traders just as prediction market exchanges and offshore venues start to offer more contracts in the oldest corner of the derivatives market.

title: Australia Home Prices Grow at Slowest Pace Since Early 2025
summary: Australian home prices rose last month at the slowest pace since January 2025, weighed down by large declines in Sydney and Melbourne as rising borrowing costs hurt demand.

title: India’s Heat Exposes a Fragile Grid as Energy Crunch Deepens
summary: India is likely to endure a blistering summer until monsoon rains arrive in June, with higher-than-average heat already straining power grids at a time when the country is grappling with energy shortages.
